steps.title: Recognize the exponential pattern
steps.content: The bacteria doubles every day. This is exponential growth, not linear. When a quantity multiplies by a constant factor each time period, use the exponential form: $$y = a \cdot b^t$$ where $a$ is the initial amount and $b$ is the growth factor (the multiplier each period).

steps.title: Identify the initial amount and growth factor
steps.content:
From the problem:
  • Initial bacteria at $t = 0$: $a = 72,000$
  • Growth factor per day: $b = 2$ (doubles means multiply by 2)
Substitute into the exponential form: $$y = 72,000 \cdot 2^t$$

steps.title: Verify with a test value and match the choice
steps.content:
Check: At $t = 1$ day, $y = 72,000(2)^1 = 144,000$ bacteria — exactly double the initial 72,000. ✓

The equation $y = 72,000(2)^t$ matches choice D — D is correct.
